shellCommands.command: /bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,220p' AGENTS.md && printf '\\n--- status ---\\n' && git status --short && printf '\\n--- day references ---\\n' && rg -n \"in-selecting-range|selectingRange|isInSelectingRange|month\" src/day.tsx src/month.tsx src/calendar.tsx src/test/day_test.test.tsx src/test/multi_month_test.test.tsx"
/bin/bash -lc 'mempal search "react-datepicker selectsRange multi-month filler day in-selecting-range highlight" --json --top-k 5'
/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1540,1745p' src/test/day_test.test.tsx && sed -n '1160,1270p' src/test/day_test.test.tsx && sed -n '450,530p' src/month.tsx && sed -n '1000,1065p' src/calendar.tsx"
/bin/bash -lc "node -e \"const p=require('./package.json'); console.log(JSON.stringify(p.scripts,null,2))\" && git diff --check && git diff -- src/day.tsx src/test/day_test.test.tsx"
/bin/bash -lc 'git diff --check && git status --short && git diff --stat && git diff -- src/day.tsx src/test/day_test.test.tsx'

/bin/bash -lc 'cat > "$HOME/.mempal-notes/react-datepicker-range-preview.md" <<'"'EOF'
# Keep range previews local to the displayed month

Month grids render adjacent-month dates as filler cells, so day-level selecting-range logic must reject dates identified as outside the displayed month before applying preview classes. Reusing the day component's existing adjacent-month predicates also preserves standalone day behavior where no displayed-month context is supplied.
EOF
mempal ingest \""'$HOME/.mempal-notes" --wing memory-evals'
